history.subject: ℙ¹ chart data (WIP): coordinate away-fractions Xⱼ/Xᵢ and their pulled-back chart sections x=X₁/X₀, y=X₀/X₁

Curve-to-P1 finiteness (node N9): a nonconstant k-morphism from the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve to a separated finite-type k-scheme is finite — dim(stalk) <= 1 by unramified/quasi-finite descent of rel-dim-1 standard-smooth charts to k[X], hence non-generic points are closed and proper closed subsets finite; fibres are finite via closed-points-to-closed-points, properness by cancellation, and Zariski's Main Theorem closes IsFinite. HasFiniteMapToP1 is now derived from the single strictly weaker gate ExistsNonconstantMapToP1
ℙ¹ chart span core: degree-(a+b) monomial fraction X₀ᵃX₁ᵇ/X₀^(a+b) = (X₁/X₀)ᵇ in (ℤ[X]_{X₀})₀
ℙ¹ chart data: document landed lemmas (coordinate fractions, sections, monomial span heart) and remaining route

Bridge IsLocallyTrivial → IsInvertibleGr: a locally trivial line bundle is invertible-graded (trivialising affine charts form a basis; each carries an invertible section module via restrictAppIso), giving the serreTwist family IsInvertibleGr instances that unlock the graded-commutative section ring Γ_*(X,O(m))

D5: finitely generated graded module over MvPolynomial(Fin r) κ (degree-one polyModule action) has finite-dimensional graded pieces — monomial/homogeneous degree-shift from RaisesDegree, then the degree-n projection of p·h lands in the finite-dimensional image of restrictTotalDegree n under ·•h; discharges the [∀ n, FiniteDimensional κ (ℳ n)] hypothesis of the Hilbert-series induction

Adelic chi-ledger start (N13 core): the coboundary subgroup B(D) = Gamma(U0,O(D)) + Gamma(U1,O(D)) inside the function field, the twist-ledger Submodule exactness at the L-terms for D <= D', and the local-step quotient scaffolding — the elementary lattice algebra of Weil's chi additivity over the 2-chart cover. (One noncomputable annotation added post-wave to pass kernel check.)

Register the wave-6 modules: FiniteMapToP1 (nonconstant curve morphisms to separated finite-type targets are finite; HasFiniteMapToP1 reduced to the bare ExistsNonconstantMapToP1 gate), P1ChartData (coordinate sections + monomial span core), ChiLedger (N13 twist-ledger core), InvertibleGrBridge (IsLocallyTrivial => IsInvertibleGr, unlocking the commutative section graded ring for line bundles), GradedPiecesFinite (f.g. graded modules over MvPolynomial have finite-dimensional pieces, Serre D5).

Picard/SchematicSupport: descent-iso affine heart — for I ≤ Ann_R M the closed-immersion unit m ↦ 1⊗ₜm : M → (R⧸I)⊗_R M is bijective (M ≃ₗ (R⧸I)⊗M), the sections-level iso step of F ≅ i_*i^*F needing only annihilator_ideal_le, not the blocked sharpness reverse

Reduce nonconstant-map gate to base-change-free Proj-model gate: ExistsNonconstantMapToP1 C follows from a nonconstant morphism C ⟶ Proj ℤ[X₀,X₁] via pullback.lift over the terminal object (ℙ¹_k = Spec k ×_⊤ Proj), discharging the over-k/base-change bookkeeping and isolating the residual curve theory in ExistsNonconstantMapToProjInt
